title: AT2PO (Agentic Turn-based Policy Optimization via Tree Search)

defines.name: AT2PO
defines.definition: A unified framework for multi-turn agentic reinforcement learning that combines turn-level tree search, turn-wise reward propagation, and turn-based policy optimization.

defines.name: Entropy-Guided Tree Expansion
defines.definition: A tree expansion strategy that prioritizes uncertain turns to improve exploration diversity and rollout quality.

defines.name: Turn-wise Credit Assignment
defines.definition: A mechanism that propagates sparse outcome rewards backward through a turn-level tree to produce fine-grained per-turn value and advantage estimates.

defines.name: Agentic Turn-based Policy Optimization
defines.definition: A turn-level policy optimization objective that applies importance sampling and clipping at the natural turn granularity of agentic interactions.

problem: Existing agentic reinforcement learning methods suffer from limited exploration diversity, sparse credit assignment, and a mismatch between turn-based interaction structure and flat policy optimization objectives.
